focus: Recurring developer growth, launch, docs, community, and distribution loops.

agent_submissions.notes: Pilot agent 1 for Developer Distribution Tools. Focus on repeated domain loops, not generic tooling.

agent_brief.focus: Recurring developer growth, launch, docs, community, and distribution loops.
agent_brief.distribution_lens: Prefer domains that ambitious online people would use, share, debate, or brag about on X.
candidate_requirements: Return 1-3 candidate domain chips only
Every candidate must describe a repeated specialization loop
Every candidate must describe a repeated mastery loop
Prefer domains with talkability, social spread, or obvious creator / builder pull
Reject personas, generic features, and vague workflow ideas

raw_candidates.label: Changelog to Launch Loop
raw_candidates.description: A developer workflow for converting product changes into repeated launch posts, release notes, and distribution assets.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: running the repeated changelog to launch loop workflow inside the developer distribution tools wedge
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which inputs, timing, and execution patterns make Changelog to Launch Loop outperform generic or manual approaches for this audience over repeated cycles
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: review the next live signal, draft the next changelog to launch loop move, execute it, measure the response, and update the working playbook for the next cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: Many dev tools ship constantly but fail to translate that shipping energy into visible growth moments.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Many dev tools ship constantly but fail to translate that shipping energy into visible growth moments.

raw_candidates.label: Repo Proof Loop

raw_candidates.specialization_surface: running the repeated repo proof loop workflow inside the developer distribution tools wedge
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which inputs, timing, and execution patterns make Repo Proof Loop outperform generic or manual approaches for this audience over repeated cycles
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: review the next live signal, draft the next repo proof loop move, execute it, measure the response, and update the working playbook for the next cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: Open-source growth often depends on making momentum visible, not just improving the code privately.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Open-source growth often depends on making momentum visible, not just improving the code privately.

agent_brief.distribution_lens: Prefer domains that ambitious online people would use, share, debate, or brag about on X.
candidate_requirements: Return 1-3 candidate domain chips only
Every candidate must describe a repeated specialization loop
Every candidate must describe a repeated mastery loop
Prefer domains with talkability, social spread, or obvious creator / builder pull
Reject personas, generic features, and vague workflow ideas

raw_candidates.label: Changelog Cycle Tool
raw_candidates.description: A recurring developer distribution tools workflow for changelog and cycle decisions that compounds through repeated cycles.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: running the repeated changelog cycle tool workflow inside the developer distribution tools wedge
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which inputs, timing, and execution patterns make Changelog Cycle Tool outperform generic or manual approaches for this audience over repeated cycles
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: review the next live signal, draft the next changelog cycle tool move, execute it, measure the response, and update the working playbook for the next cycle
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: Developer teams repeatedly face changelog and cycle decisions, but most still improvise them manually.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Developer teams repeatedly face changelog and cycle decisions, but most still improvise them manually.
